title: Gouania retinaria
intro: Gouania retinaria, also known as the Retinaria tree, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Africa. The health benefits of Gouania retinaria include its antimalarial, antimicrobial, and antihemorrhagic properties, which make it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gouania retinaria is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also made into a syrup, to treat various conditions, including malaria and hemorrhages.
taxonomy: Gouania retinaria belongs to the family Rhamnaceae and the order Rosales. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Gouania. Within the Rhamnaceae family, it is classified as a member of the tribe Gouanieae. Further classification places Gouania retinaria as a member of the subtribe Gouaniinae. The overall classification suggests a unique position within the Rhamnaceae family.
